title: webhook key compare leaks key length via early exit
description: webhook.rs lines 50-51 short-circuit on length before the constant-time compare: if expected_bytes.len() != provided_bytes.len() { return UNAUTHORIZED }. This leaks the expected key length to timing measurement — an attacker can binary-search the key length in O(log n) probes before the ct_eq even runs. The fix introduced in pass 1 preserved the oracle for length. A correct fix either: (a) uses HMAC-based comparison (hmac::verify), or (b) always runs ct_eq on length-normalized inputs (pad shorter side to longer, then compare, discarding result when lengths differ only after the ct_eq finishes). The subtle::ConstantTimeEq trait handles equal-length inputs only; mismatched lengths must be handled by the caller without branching on the difference.
close_reason: Pass 3 fixes: HMAC key compare, inbox SSRF guard, domain validation, async Accept dispatch, foreign keys, IPv6 SSRF, poll timeout. Remaining P2-P3 accepted as known debt.
